description_long: This is a large, upright stone fragment with a roughly hewn surface and a partially preserved human torso sculpted in relief. The front displays the remains of a draped garment, with the right arm and shoulder more defined, while the left side is broken and jagged. The back of the stone features a vertical panel of deeply incised hieroglyphic inscriptions, suggesting it was once part of a monumental statue or stela. The stone's color ranges from dark gray to brown, with lighter patches at the base where the surface is chipped or worn. The texture is predominantly rough and uneven, with some smoother areas where the original carving remains intact. This object would likely be encountered as a historical artifact or decorative feature in an urban park, plaza, or museum garden.
description: A large, upright stone fragment with a sculpted torso in relief and hieroglyphic inscriptions on the back. The surface is rough and weathered, with dark gray and brown tones.
description_view_0: The front view shows the sculpted torso with draped garment details and a jagged, broken left edge.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the stone's narrow profile and the layered, chipped texture along the edge.
description_view_2: The back view highlights a vertical panel of hieroglyphic inscriptions running down the center.
description_view_3: The right side view displays the smoother contour of the torso and the transition to the rougher, broken surfaces.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on a paved or landscaped area
attribute_pattern: Deeply incised hieroglyphic carvings on the back, with irregular chisel marks and natural stone mottling elsewhere
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered with chipped edges and some smoother, worn surfaces from exposure
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with manicured lawns or paved surfaces, often highlighted as a focal point or surrounded by low barriers
